name: aws-clean
description: Remove expired named profiles from ~/.aws/credentials. Protected profiles (sea, decaf, expeso, cc) are never removed. Uses `aws configure get expiration` — same mechanism as aws-expiry — so it works regardless of which field name saml2aws writes to the credentials file.
streamdeck_notes: needs argument(s) — use Stream Deck property inspector text field. Usage: aws-clean [--dry-run]

name: ghissues
description: Interactive GitHub issue browser (Textual TUI), launched via uv.
streamdeck_notes: interactive full-screen TUI — good as a 'launch app in terminal' button, not a run-and-forget action

name: history-analyze
description: Analyzes shell history for context reconstruction, patterns, and productivity insights.
streamdeck_notes: several no-arg subcommands (summary, today, patterns, timeline) are great direct-key candidates

name: notify-input-needed
description: Sends a desktop notification when a shell command is waiting on input; called by a precmd hook, not by hand.
streamdeck_notes: internal plumbing invoked by shell hooks — skip

name: notify-verify
description: Verifies the input-notification system is correctly installed and loaded.
streamdeck_notes: no args, diagnostic check — decent 'health check' button candidate

name: repo-discovery.sh
description: Scans ~/GithubRepositories and ~/GitRepositories, builds a TSV cache of repos + worktrees.
streamdeck_notes: no required args — good 'refresh repo cache' button

name: nvim-help
description: fzf-driven browser over extracted Neovim keymaps/config (nvim-helper package).
streamdeck_notes: interactive fzf browser — launch-only button

name: nvim-work
description: Creates/attaches a 'nvim-focused-work' tmux session with nvim + nvim-help + terminal panes.
streamdeck_notes: no args, idempotent (attaches if exists) — excellent 'start my work session' button, this is exactly the kind of thing this Stream Deck plugin's TMUX_SESSIONS is for

name: tmux-daily-note.sh
description: Opens today's Obsidian daily note in nvim (falls back to ~/notes/daily-<date>.md).
streamdeck_notes: no args, opens editor on today's note — strong direct-key candidate

name: tmux-hud.sh
description: Prints a small dashboard: current session, git branch/status, active task, focus-timer remaining.
streamdeck_notes: no args, read-only status view — good 'show status' button (output shown in a pane/popup)

name: tmux-next-attention.sh
description: Jumps to the next tmux window flagged as needing attention (notification system).
streamdeck_notes: no args — good direct-key candidate for quick triage

name: tmux-sessionizer.sh
description: fzf-driven interactive tmux session manager: list/switch/create sessions from repo directories.
streamdeck_notes: interactive fzf picker — launch-only button (open a terminal running this)

name: metadata-parser.py
description: Library: parses/aggregates the [type:*][task:*][context:*] structured commit message format. Not a standalone CLI.
streamdeck_notes: library module, not directly invokable — skip

name: copilot-squash.py
description: Aggregates metadata commits in a range into one semantic conventional-commit message via Copilot CLI.
streamdeck_notes: requires a commit-range arg tied to current repo state — property-inspector text field, situational use

name: squash-helper.sh
description: Orchestrates squashing metadata commits: calls copilot-squash.py, performs git ops, rolls back on failure.
streamdeck_notes: needs commit-range arg; mainly invoked automatically by the pre-push hook, not by hand — low priority

name: pre-push (git hook template)
description: Git pre-push hook: detects metadata-format commits being pushed and auto-invokes squash-helper before allowing the push.
usage: installed as .git/hooks/pre-push, runs automatically on 'git push'
streamdeck_notes: not directly invoked by a user action — skip

name: gh_board.py
description: LazyGit-style terminal GitHub board: your issues/PRs to review, PRs you opened, auto-refreshing.
streamdeck_notes: interactive full-screen board — launch-only button, great 'open my GitHub board' key

name: vscode-launcher.sh
description: Launches VS Code or VS Code Insiders, picking whichever is available.
streamdeck_notes: already covered conceptually by APP_LAUNCHERS in dev-workflow.config.ts; useful if you want the insiders/stable fallback logic specifically

name: test-rules.sh (codex)
description: Validates Codex CLI execpolicy rules by running test commands against them.
streamdeck_notes: no required args — decent 'validate codex rules' diagnostic button

name: show-category.py / show-category.sh (shortcuts)
description: Displays all keyboard shortcuts for a given category from the unified shortcuts.json database (also used by Alfred).
streamdeck_notes: shows a menu if no category given, otherwise prints a category directly — could wire one button per category (e.g. 'navigation')

name: alfred-search.py / alfred-search.sh
description: Alfred Script Filter backend for the unified shortcuts database (fuzzy search over shortcuts.json).
streamdeck_notes: designed for Alfred's script-filter protocol (JSON out), not a standalone terminal action — skip

name: parse-shortcuts.py
description: Parses UNIFIED_KEYBOARD_SHORTCUTS.md into the structured shortcuts.json database.
streamdeck_notes: maintenance/build step for the shortcuts DB, run after editing the source markdown — 'rebuild shortcuts db' button

name: markdown-reader.zsh (mdread family)
description: Text-to-speech functions for markdown docs: mdread, mdread-clean, mdread-summary, mdread-keypoints, mdread-simple, mdread-list, mdread-find, mdread-recover.
streamdeck_notes: mdread-list / mdread-find are no-arg and good direct keys; the rest need a file path — property-inspector text field

name: copilot-zsh-history-analyzer.sh
description: Analyzes zsh history for command patterns vs. existing functions and generates AI-powered automation proposals via Copilot.
usage: copilot-zsh-history-analyzer.sh [--days N] [--history-file PATH] [--output PATH] [--model NAME] [--max-samples N] [--verbose]
streamdeck_notes: all flags have defaults, runnable with zero args — good 'find new automation ideas' button, but slow/AI-call heavy so best as an occasional-use key

name: arc/install.sh
description: Installs & configures Arc browser for the dev workflow.
streamdeck_notes: setup/idempotent-install script — not a repeatable Stream Deck action; listed for completeness
